---
title: "Module 2 — Generalization"
format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
    toc: false
---

<!-- Thin module landing (repo convention: index.qmd is a landing, never a
     lecture deck). Decks live beside it; see CLAUDE.md. -->

How well will a fitted model do on patients it has never seen? Training error
versus generalization error, $k$-fold cross-validation, the bias–variance
decomposition behind the U-shaped test-error curve, and ridge regression as the
knob that moves you along it.

| Deck | Link |
|------|------|
| Lecture A — Evaluation & Cross-Validation | [m2a-evaluation-cv](m2a-evaluation-cv.html) |
| Lecture B — Bias–Variance & Regularization | [m2b-bias-variance-ridge](m2b-bias-variance-ridge.html) |
| Appendix — Bias–Variance decomposition *(forthcoming; see CLAUDE.md backlog)* | — |

Companion activity: [week02 bias-variance / ridge explorer](https://gchism94.github.io/info521-activities-2026/week02-bias-variance/)
(embedded live in Lecture B).

[← All slide decks](../../index.html)
